target.name: Thinking Machines Lab
target.founder: Mira Murati, John Schulman, Barrett Zoph, Lilian Weng

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $300B+

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Thinking Machines Lab is a seed-stage AI & Software company seeking valuation support. The analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: OpenAI, Liquid AI, Field AI, Sakana AI, and Twelve Labs, representing a broader universe of nine private and one public company (Palantir) in the frontier AI space.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier to the company's last post-money valuation of $12.0B, implying $30.0B at the next funding stage. Peer-Median analysis yields $2.0B, calculated as the median valuation across the five comparables (range: $250M to $300.0B). The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in company maturity and capital intensity within the AI sector.

The triangulated range spans $1.7B (low) to $34.5B (high), with a median of $16.0B. The current stated valuation of $12.0B sits within this band, below the median estimate. Key uncertainties include the reliability of peer valuations given extreme outliers in the comp set—notably OpenAI at $300.0B—and whether stage-progression multiples appropriately capture this company's specific trajectory and capital efficiency. The wide range reflects genuine ambiguity in pricing early-stage frontier AI ventures amid volatile market sentiment and limited precedent for comparable exits.
